title: Designing new ad units for passengers, in-flight
label: Viasat Ad Products
description: As part of Visual Logic, I worked closely with the Viasat Ad Product team to design a suite of ad products for the near-term and long-term vision of the product.
intro: Viasat is a satellite internet provider that provides internet to 250 million passengers across 60+ airlines globally. As a UX Designer at Visual Logic, I worked with Viasat's advertising product team to build out their emerging product — advertising in-flight.

sections.heading: The Approach
sections.body:
As Viasat scales their ad product, brands and advertisers expect more and more capabilities.

A survey unit offers a window to customer data that was previously impossible to collect in-flight.

sections.heading: The Data
sections.body:
Advertisers often launch surveys in order to gather data on brand perception — so a survey unit folds in perfectly across Viasat's existing product as a premium offering to a campaign.

The survey unit's placement is at the critical moment in a captive audience's engagement: the waiting period before connecting.

sections.heading: The Solution
sections.body: Through testing, the value of the passenger data increases with how easy it is to use — if there's a clear path forward, passengers are more likely to be honest in responses, rather than rage clicking their way through and compromising data.

items.label: Increased potential revenue
items.body: Premium 'add-on' framework for existing advertising products, creating a new high-tier inventory for airline partners.

items.label: Boosted passenger engagement
items.body: Interactive, choice-based ad unit as a high-performance alternative to traditional video-driven experiences.

items.label: Matured targeting data
items.body: Survey-focused ad unit generating actionable user insights that drive future content and product development.
